heading: Jurisdiction and control; authorization of annual appropriation
label: 429a Jurisdiction and control; authorization of annual appropriation
text: Each parcel of land acquired under shall be under the jurisdiction and control of the Secretary of the Interior, and there is authorized to be appropriated for the maintenance of each such parcel of land, fence, approach, and marker a sum not to exceed $250 per annum.section 429 of this title
notes: Transfer of Functions“Secretary of the Interior” substituted in text for “Secretary of War” pursuant to Reorg. Plan No. 3 of 1950, §§ 1, 2; Ex. Ord. No. 6166, § 2; and Ex. Ord. No. 6228, § 1. See below.Reorg. Plan No. 3 of 1950, §§ 1, 2, eff. , 15 F.R. 3174, , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees, transferred the functions of other officers, employees, and agencies of the Department of the Interior, with certain exceptions, to the Secretary of the Interior, with power to delegate.May 24, 195064 Stat. 1262Ex. Ord. No. 6166, § 2, and Ex. Ord. No. 6228, § 1, set out as a note under , transferred the administrative functions of Brices Cross Roads and Tupelo battlefield sites to the Department of the Interior.section 901 of Title 5
